channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, returns to Instagram and posts a New Year's video
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, is starting the new year with her first Instagram account since 2020. The Associated Press confirmed Wednesday that @meghan belongs to the Duchess of Sussex.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 1 dead, 7 injured in Tesla fire, explosion at Las Vegas Trump International Hotel; sources say it wasn't an accident
item.description: A car fire at the Trump International Hotel in Las Vegas caused smoke and loud booms, disrupting guests and nearby residents.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, NewsNation

item.title: Power is restored to nearly all of Puerto Rico after a major blackout
item.description: Electric power has been restored to nearly all affected customers across Puerto Rico after a sweeping blackout plunged the U.S. territory into darkness on New Year’s Eve.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Pérez Pintado, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sugar Bowl postponed to Thursday night
item.description: Hours following the New Year's Day tragedy on Bourbon Street, Wednesday's Allstate Sugar Bowl bout between Notre Dame and Georgia is being postponed.
item.dc:creator: Richie Mills, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 1 person dies when Tesla Cybertruck catches fire and explodes outside Trump's Las Vegas hotel
item.description: Authorities say one person died and seven others people were injured when a Tesla Cybertruck caught fire and exploded outside President-elect Donald Trump’s Las Vegas hotel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The world welcomes 2025 with light shows, embraces and ice plunges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — From Sydney to Mumbai to Paris to Rio de Janeiro, communities around the world welcomed 2025 with spectacular light shows, embraces and ice plunges.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, CEDAR ATTANASIO and CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The Latest: Death toll in New Orleans crash rises to 15, coroner says
item.description: A driver behind the wheel of a pickup truck rammed into a crowd of New Orleans revelers on Bourbon Street early on New Year’s Day, killing at least 15 people in what the FBI is investigating as an act of terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Family’s SUV stolen with senior dog inside
item.description: An Illinois family is pleading for answers and any information that could lead to the safe return of their beloved dog.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Spinelli, NewsNation

item.title: Commission to weigh offer to settle 'Jane Doe' suit involving Sean Williams
item.description: The city's legal department recommends "consideration and approval" in a brief item in the agenda packet.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Keeling, NewsNation

item.title: New York employers must now offer paid medical leave during pregnancy
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — Pregnant New Yorkers will be entitled to at least 20 hours of paid leave to attend prenatal medical appointments under a law that took effect Wednesday. Gov. Kathy Hochul said the policy makes New York the first state in the country to offer paid leave for prenatal care.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FBI finds 150 homemade bombs at Virginia home in one of the largest such seizures, prosecutors say
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Federal agents found one of the largest stockpiles of homemade explosives they have ever seized when they arrested a Virginia man on a firearms charge last month, according to a court filing by federal prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 7 rising Democratic leaders to watch
item.description: Democrats are scrambling for a new leader to head the party and organize resistance to President-elect Trump following Vice President Harris’s loss in November's election.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he'll attend Jimmy Carter's funeral
item.description: Asked by The Associated Press as he walked into a New Year's Eve party at his Mar-a-Lago club in Florida, Trump said "I'll be there.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: FDA testing aged raw cow's milk cheese for bird flu
item.description: Raw, unpasteurized milk is a known vector of bird flu when derived from infected cow herds. It can also contain pathogens such as E. coli, salmonella, and both Streptococcus and Staphylococcus bacteria.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, ABC 4

item.title: Driver 'hell-bent on carnage' kills 10, injures dozens in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A driver wrought carnage on New Orleans' famed French Quarter early on New Year's Day, killing 10 people as he steered around a police blockade and rammed a pickup truck into a crowd before being shot to death by police, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Suspect identified after driver kills ten, injures dozens in Bourbon Street truck attack
item.description: At least 10 people are dead after a driver plowed a car into a crowd on Bourbon Street in New Orleans and then exited the vehicle and fired a weapon, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California Highway Patrol gets in some monkey business during routine stop
item.description: A traffic stop by California Highway Patrol took an unusual turn when officials pulled over a car for excessive speeding and found an exotic animal in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Psychedelic therapy begins in Colorado, causing tension between conservatives and veterans
item.description: Colorado becomes the second state to allow psychedelic therapy this week after two years of rulemaking.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US and Boeing investigators examine the site of a deadly South Korean plane crash
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A team of U.S. investigators including representatives from Boeing on Tuesday examined the site of a plane crash that killed 179 people in South Korea while authorities were conducting safety inspections on all Boeing 737-800 aircraft operated by the country’s airlines.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New California law takes aim at homework burden on students
item.description: You may remember spending hours after school doing homework. A lawmaker in California is hope to change that for the next generation of students in the state.
item.dc:creator: Eytan Wallace, NewsNation

item.title: NYE drone show canceled on Folly Beach after drones fall from sky; at least 1 injured
item.description: FOLLY BEACH, S.C. (WCBD) - A new drone show on Folly Beach was canceled Tuesday night after the drones malfunctioned and fell from the sky. Organizers completed two drone displays before they all went down, News 2 learned.
item.dc:creator: Tim Renaud, Fox 5

item.title: Chief Justice John Roberts defends judicial independence, says it is under threat in several ways
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chief Justice John Roberts issued a defense Tuesday of judicial independence, which he said is under threat from intimidation, disinformation and the prospect of public officials defying court orders. Roberts laid out his concerns in his annual report on the federal judiciary.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge rules on NYC congestion pricing; interpretation differs between parties
item.description: A U.S. District Court Judge in New Jersey ruled on New York's plan to impose congestion pricing in Manhattan, though the interpretation differs between both parties in the suit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The killing of an 18-year-old Ohio woman was solved with DNA technology after 43 years
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — A man who was shot dead last month as authorities attempted to serve him an indictment on federal gun charges has been identified as the killer of an 18-year-old Ohio woman in a case that had gone unsolved for 43 years, police announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Angelina Jolie, Brad Pitt reach divorce settlement after 8 years
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Angelina Jolie and Brad Pitt have reached a divorce settlement, ending one of the longest and most contentious divorces in Hollywood history but not every legal issue between the two.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Jimmy Carter made eradicating Guinea worm disease a top mission
item.description: JARWENG, South Sudan (AP) — Nobel Prize-winning peacemaker Jimmy Carter spent nearly four decades waging war to eliminate an ancient parasite plaguing the world’s poorest people.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI issues warning after home of NBA star Luka Doncic targeted in string of burglaries
item.description: The FBI is warning sports leagues about crime organizations targeting professional athletes following a string of burglaries at the homes of prominent NFL and NBA players.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, Fox 5

item.title: FBI issues warning after home of NBA star Luka Doncic targeted in string of burglaries
item.description: The FBI is warning sports leagues about crime organizations targeting professional athletes following a string of burglaries at the homes of prominent NFL and NBA players.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Loo, WGN 9

item.title: Census Bureau releases 'population clock' projection for New Year's Day
item.description: Based on current estimates, there will also be one birth every 9 seconds and one death every 9.4 seconds in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KRON4

item.title: How to watch WWE's 'Raw' on Netflix: What to know before the Jan. 6 debut
item.description: After 21 years, WWE's "RAW" is leaving cable to join Netflix every Monday night beginning Jan. 6, meaning streaming is the only way to watch the longest-running weekly episodic show.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, Fox 5

item.title: UnitedHealthcare taught us ways to deny claims: Former employee
item.description: Collins described crying at her desk while handling calls from desperate patients, as supervisors laughed.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Argentine judge charges 5 people over death of former One Direction star Liam Payne
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — An Argentine judge has confirmed charges against five people in connection with the death of Liam Payne, a former member of musical group One Direction. The judge has ordered preventive prison for two of them for having supplied Payne with drugs.
item.dc:creator: ALMUDENA CALATRAVA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: South Korea struggles to find cause of plane crash that killed 179
item.description: South Korean officials said Monday they will conduct safety inspections of all Boeing 737-800 aircraft operated by the country's airlines as they struggle to determine what caused a plane crash that killed 179 people a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US children are struggling with reading
item.description: Learning to read can become exponentially more difficult for older students and adults who have fallen behind, creating grave concerns as the number of individuals struggling with literacy into high school and adulthood is increasing.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
